steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage concludes that the ability to distinguish spontaneous from volitional laughter is universal across cultures.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B reports that participants correctly identified laugh types at a rate well above chance and that this success rate was similar in Austria, Samoa, and the other societies, showing a cross‑cultural ability to distinguish laughter types.
Choices A, C, and D either look at unrelated variables such as economic conditions or acoustic features, or do not demonstrate that recognition rates are alike across cultures.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
